patterns.name: "not just X — it's Y" antithesis
patterns.why: Default antithesis tic; concedes a position nobody held to sound nuanced.

patterns.name: trailing significance participle
patterns.why: Participle clause manufactures analysis while adding no information (2-5x human rate).

patterns.name: rule-of-three list
patterns.why: Compulsive triads; flag only when clustered/regular.

patterns.name: formulaic opener
patterns.regex: \b(in today'?s (fast-paced|digital|modern)|in the (world|realm) of|at its core|when it comes to)\b

patterns.name: announcement-post cliché
patterns.why: Announcement-post boilerplate; hollow enthusiasm carrying no information.

patterns.name: puffery / significance phrase
patterns.why: Encyclopedic puffery; asserts importance without evidence (Wikipedia 'Signs of AI writing').

patterns.name: ChatGPT-overused emoji
patterns.why: Emoji ChatGPT inserts ~10x the human rate in prose (WaPo analysis of 330k messages).

patterns.name: hype / marketing emoji
patterns.why: Startup/announcement emoji cliché; also common in genuine human social copy, so weighted low and damped outside marketing.

patterns.name: emoji used as a list bullet
patterns.why: Emoji as a bullet or section marker — an AI formatting tell regardless of which glyph.

patterns.name: em-dash (contextual, off by default)
patterns.why: Em-dash overuse is a weak, model-dependent tell with high false-positive risk; off by default, flagged only when you opt in via .plainly.toml.
